reasoning: The Cargill Kitchen Solutions recall was issued on March 28, 2025 for liquid egg products that have 'Use By' dates of August 9 and August 10, 2025. Standard USDA FSIS procedure is to keep a recall 'Active' until the recalling firm has retrieved the product and resolved the root cause, but fundamentally, recalls typically remain open at least until the products have passed their marked expiration dates to protect consumers holding the product. Since the resolution date of July 1, 2025 is more than a month before the product expiration date, it is highly unlikely FSIS will change the status to 'Closed' by then. Even for Class III recalls, the typical administrative timeline to close a recall file stretches beyond three months. Therefore, the probability of it resolving 'Yes' is very low.
